attributes.description: Alerts when a Kubernetes deployment has fewer available replicas than desired, indicating the deployment cannot maintain its target replica count. Common causes: rolling update failures, resource starvation, image pull errors.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Deployment unavailable replicas

investigation_guide.blob:
## Deployment Unavailable Replicas

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected deployment(s) from the alert results.
2. Check the Workload Health dashboard for deployment status.
3. Run `kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>` to check events and conditions.
4. Look for pods in non-Running states: `kubectl get pods -l app=<deployment> -n <namespace>`.
5. Navigate to Pod & Container Detail filtered by the namespace to inspect failing pods.

### Common Causes
- Image pull errors (wrong tag, private registry auth)
- Insufficient cluster resources (CPU, memory) to schedule pods
- Pod crash loops preventing readiness
- Rolling update stuck due to new version failures
- PVC binding failures for pods requiring persistent storage

### Tuning
- The `alertDelay` of 5 runs allows for brief transient gaps during rolling updates.
- Increase `alertDelay` if your deployments have slow startup times.
- The rule uses `desired > 0` to exclude scaled-to-zero deployments.

### Escalation
If a deployment remains degraded for more than 10 minutes, investigate the underlying pod failures and consider rolling back.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.deployment.desired IS NOT NULL
    AND k8s.deployment.available IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    desired = LAST(k8s.deployment.desired, @timestamp),
    available = LAST(k8s.deployment.available, @timestamp)
    BY k8s.deployment.name, k8s.namespace.name
// Only alert for deployments that should have replicas running
| WHERE desired > 0
// Alert when available replicas are fewer than desired
| WHERE available < desired
| EVAL unavailable = desired - available
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.deployment.name, desired, available, unavailable
| SORT unavailable DESC
